# Test Ad 5: "One Big Fix Away"

**Source:** Crafted — strong everywhere except CTA/offer is completely broken
**Expected quality band:** Near-great but broken (~65-75) — strong R, M, B, but critically flawed CTA
**Tests:** Triage value — B should show 1 Rewrite + minor fixes; A just shows 3 recs

---

## Ad Copy

If you're a DTC brand owner spending $50K+ a month on Meta ads, I need to tell you something uncomfortable:

Your creative isn't the problem. Your audiences aren't the problem. Your pixel data is fine.

The problem is your landing page is leaking money like a cracked bucket.

I've audited over 340 DTC landing pages in the last 18 months, and I keep finding the same conversion killer: what I call "The Friction Stack."

Here's what The Friction Stack looks like:

Your hero section promises one thing. Your product description promises something different. Your testimonials talk about a third thing entirely. And your FAQ section is answering questions nobody asked.

It's not that any single element is bad. It's that they're all pulling in different directions — creating cognitive friction that makes visitors bounce before they ever see your Add to Cart button.

The fix is something I call "Message Threading." Instead of treating each section as an independent block, you create a single persuasive thread that weaves through every element on the page. The hero sets up the mechanism. The product section delivers the proof. The testimonials reinforce the specific claim. And the FAQ handles the exact objections your mechanism creates.

When Hydra Supplements implemented Message Threading on their Ashwagandha PDP, their conversion rate went from 2.1% to 4.7% — without changing their traffic source, their offer, or their price point. That's a 124% increase in revenue from the same ad spend.

BarkBox did something similar with their subscription landing page and saw a 89% lift in trial-to-paid conversion after threading their "surprise and delight" mechanism through every section.

I've packaged everything I know about Message Threading into a comprehensive system.

For more information, please visit our website or reach out to our team through the appropriate channels to discuss how we might be able to explore potential collaboration opportunities in this space.
